title: Autologous mitochondrial extraction and expansion
abstract: This invention creates a new paradigm for the treatment of age related conditions. As a consequence of aging, mutations arise in the genome of mitochondria in the body, which lessens their function as power providers for the cells in which they reside. To overcome this deficit, this disclosure provides new ways of preparing rejuvenated mitochondria that are tailored for administration to a subject who has manifest signs of an age related condition. Autologous mitochondria can be obtained, replicated (for example, in a producer cell line), and sorted for enhanced mitochondrial function such as capacity to generate ATP. The mitochondria are then transplanted back into the subject, with a view to rejuvenating energy generation capacity and ameliorating the signs and symptoms of the age-related condition.
claims: Claims CLAIMS The invention claimed is: 1. A method for preparing a medicament containing mitochondria, wherein the medicament is configured for improving mitochondrial function in a subject in need thereof, the method comprising: obtaining a sample that includes originating eukaryotic cells containing mitochondria, wherein the cells are autologous to the subject; optionally replicating the originating cells obtained from the sample; optionally isolating mitochondria from the originating cells; optionally replicating the isolated mitochondria by proliferating a population of producer cells that have been implanted with the isolated mitochondria; selecting a subset of mitochondria from the originating cells or the producer cells that have improved mitochondrial function compared with mitochondria in the sample, wherein the selecting is done before or after the mitochondria have been isolated and/or replicated; and formulating the selected mitochondria or cells containing the selected mitochondria for administration back to the subject. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the improved mitochondrial function is an improved ability to produce ATP under aerobic conditions, a higher mitochondrial volume fraction, or a higher membrane potential. 3. The method of either claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the selecting of the subset of mitochondria with improved mitochondrial function is performed by automated sorting of individual mitochondria or by automated sorting of cells that contain the mitochondria. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the individual mitochondria or the cells are sorted using a dye, the intensity of which reflects ATP production or membrane potential of the mitochondria, or by clonal sorting of the cells that contain the mitochondria using an assay for mitochondrial function and/or mRNA heterogeneity. 5. The method of any preceding claim, whereby mitochondria are rejuvenated during proliferation of the originating cells or the producer cells so as to have an improved ability to produce ATP under aerobic conditions. 6. The method of any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the originating eukaryotic cells has been obtained from a biological sample taken from the subject, such as a sample of bone marrow. 7. The method of any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the originating eukaryotic cells has been obtained by implanting previously obtained mitochondria autologous to the subject into a producer cell population which has optionally been treated to remove endogenous mitochondria. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the originating eukaryotic cells are genetically altered or otherwise reprogrammed to increase proliferative capacity. 9. The method of claim 7, wherein the originating eukaryotic cells are genetically or pharmacologically altered to express or contain Oct4 (Octamer binding transcription factor-4), Sox2 (Sex determining region Y)-box 2, Klf4 (Kruppel Like Factor-4), c-Myc, a TET (ten eleven translocation methylcytosine dioxygenase), a DNA methyltransferase (DMT), telomerase reverse transcriptase (TRT), or a combination thereof. 10. The method of claim 8 or claim 9, wherein the mitochondria are replicated by proliferating the originating eukaryotic cells after the cells have been programmed or altered. 11. The method of any preceding claim, wherein the mitochondria are isolated, selected, optionally cryopreserved, and then implanted back into a population of producer cells, whereupon the mitochondria are replicated by proliferating the population of producer cells and/or by inducing mitochondrial biogenesis in the producer cells. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the population of producer cells is autologous to the subject. 13. The method of claim 1, comprising the following steps in the following order: (1) adapting cells autologous to the subject to grow in culture, thereby creating the originating eukaryotic cells; (2) reprogramming or genetically altering the originating eukaryotic cells in culture to increase proliferative capacity; (3) proliferating the reprogrammed or genetically altered cells in the culture so as to replicate and rejuvenate the mitochondria; (4) sorting the cells from the culture to obtain a subset of cells containing mitochondria that have improved mitochondrial function compared with other cells being sorted; (5) isolating mitochondria from the cells after the sorting; and (6) formulating the isolated mitochondria for administration to the subject. 14. The method of claim 1, comprising the following steps in the following order: (1) isolating mitochondria from a sample of cells obtained from the subject; (2) sorting the isolated mitochondria to obtain a subset of mitochondria that have improved mitochondrial function compared with other mitochondria being sorted; (3) implanting the subset of mitochondria that have improved mitochondrial function into a population of producer cells from which endogenous mitochondria have been removed; (4) proliferating the population of producer cells in culture so as to replicate and rejuvenate the mitochondria; (5) isolating mitochondria from the cells in the culture after the proliferating; and (6) formulating the subset of mitochondria for administration to the subject. 15. The method of claim 1, comprising the following steps in the following order: (1) adapting cells autologous to the subject to grow in culture, thereby creating the originating eukaryotic cells; (2) reprogramming or genetically altering the originating eukaryotic cells in culture to increase proliferative capacity; (3) proliferating the reprogrammed or genetically altered cells in the culture so as to replicate and rejuvenate the mitochondria; (4) isolating mitochondria from the cells in the culture after the proliferating; (5) sorting the isolated mitochondria to obtain a subset of mitochondria that have improved mitochondrial function compared with the other mitochondria being sorted; and (6) formulating the subset of mitochondria for administration to the subject. 16. A pharmaceutical composition configured for improving mitochondrial function in a subject in need thereof, comprising mitochondria that are autologous to the subject and that have on average an improved ability to produce ATP compared with mitochondria in tissues of the subject. 17. The pharmaceutical composition of claim 16, wherein the mitochondria in the composition reside in a host cell that is autologous to the subject. 18. A pharmaceutical composition configured for improving mitochondrial function in a subject in need thereof, prepared according to the method of any of claims 1 to 15. 19. A preparation of mitochondria prepared according to the method of any of claims 1 to 15, in an excipient that is protective of mitochondrial function for long-term cryopreservation. 20. A method for treating a subject in need of mitochondrial rejuvenation, comprising administering to the subject a pharmaceutical composition according to any of claims 16 to 18. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the subject has signs or symptoms of a condition characterized as having impaired mitochondrial function in a tissue in or around a site where the signs or the symptoms are manifest, and at least one of the signs or symptoms of the condition are relieved as a result of the administering of the pharmaceutical composition. 22. The method of claim 21 wherein the autologous mitochondria in the pharmaceutical composition were prepared before the signs or the symptoms were manifest in the subject, and were cryopreserved before the treating of the subject. 23. The method of any of claims 20 to 22, whereby the subject is treated for a neurodegenerative condition. 24. A preparation of mitochondria with improved ability to produce ATP under aerobic conditions for use in treatment of a neurodegenerative condition in a subject to which the mitochondria are autologous.
